fields.title: Epic: task workers inherit sandbox + approval (re-snapshot per call)
fields.description:
## Goal

Ensure `task` workers always run under sandbox + approval policies that are derived from the parent session's *current effective config at the moment of each task invocation* (even when reusing an existing `session_id`).

## Requirements

- Workers inherit parent sandbox + approval by default
- Task types may specify sandbox/approval overrides
- Overrides are clamped so workers can never be more permissive than the parent
- `session_id` reuse must not retain old permissions; permissions are recalculated per call
- Approval prompts must behave like the main agent:
  - if the parent has UI, worker approvals can be routed to that UI
  - if no UI, deny

## Primary Files

- `extensions/tau/src/task/index.ts`
- `extensions/tau/src/task/runner.ts`
- `extensions/tau/src/task/registry.ts`
- `extensions/tau/src/task/types.ts`
- `extensions/tau/src/sandbox/index.ts`
- `extensions/tau/src/sandbox/approval.ts`
- `extensions/tau/src/shared/state.ts`

## DoD

- Worker effective sandbox+approval is recalculated for every `task` call (including existing `session_id`)
- Worker cannot exceed parent permissions (filesystem/network/approval)
- Task config supports sandbox/approval fields (inherit by default)
- Unit tests cover policy clamping and session_id resnapshot semantics
- Manual QA confirms parent read-only cannot reuse an old `session_id` to gain more access
fields.close_reason: Task workers now resnapshot sandbox+approval per call, clamp to parent, route approvals via broker, and include vitest coverage
